description.en: Short-duration cash spice grown mainly for domestic consumption and a small export market. Three varietal groups cultivated in Sri Lanka: **Local (Sri Lankan) ginger** — small rhizomes, ash-white fibrous flesh, highest pungency and aroma; **Rangoon ginger** — medium-sized well-spread finger rhizomes; **Chinese ginger** — large watery pale-yellow rhizomes with low pungency. Comparative research in two Sri Lankan agro-climatic zones found **Siddha and Rangoon** varieties best suited for cultivation. Larger seed-rhizome pieces germinate and establish faster. DEA is the primary authority; major production in Kandy, Matale, Kurunegala, and wet-zone smallholdings.

scientific_name.quote: Zingiber officinale roscoe grown in Sri Lanka

duration_days.quote: Ginger is a perennial plant but grown as a short duration crop for approximately 8 months

description.quote: Local (Sri Lankan) Ginger: Rhizomes are small and the fibrous flesh is somewhat ash white in color, with pungency and aroma comparatively higher than other ginger varieties. Rangoon Ginger: Rhizomes are medium in size and with well spreaded finger rhizomes. Chinese Ginger: Rhizomes are large in size with watery flesh, pale yellow in color, with low pungency and aroma.
